findings.title: 8 of 10 API routes have zero authentication
findings.description: Queue, accounts, leads CRUD, generate, refine, send, and delete routes all require no auth. Only ingest routes are protected.
findings.exploit_scenario: Attacker discovers deployed URL, GETs /api/queue to read all prospect data + drafted emails, POSTs /api/leads/{id}/generate repeatedly to burn Anthropic API credits, POSTs /api/leads/{id}/send to mark leads as sent corrupting rep workflow.
findings.impact: Full data exposure of prospect PII and sales intel; unlimited Anthropic API cost amplification; CRM state corruption.
findings.recommendation: Add Next.js middleware.js at project root that checks a SITE_SECRET header/cookie on all /api/* routes except /api/ingest/*.

findings.title: No rate limiting on AI generation endpoints — unbounded Anthropic API spend
findings.description: POST /api/leads/{id}/generate makes 2 Anthropic API calls per request; POST /api/leads/{id}/refine makes 1. No rate limiting at code or platform level.
findings.exploit_scenario: Once auth is added, an insider or credential-holder scripts 1000 generate requests overnight → ~$30 in API costs. Without auth (finding #1), anyone can do this.
findings.impact: Uncontrolled Anthropic API spend. At 2 calls/generate, 500 requests = $15 in claude-sonnet-4 costs.
findings.recommendation: Add Vercel Edge rate limiting (vercel.json) or Upstash Ratelimit middleware: 10 generate calls per user per hour.

findings.title: Third-party CI action oven-sh/setup-bun@v2 not SHA-pinned
findings.description: Uses tag reference @v2, not a full commit SHA. Tag can be moved by the owner or a compromised account.
findings.exploit_scenario: oven-sh account or repo compromised; attacker pushes malicious setup-bun@v2 that exfiltrates repo secrets or injects backdoor into bun install artifacts. CI runs on every push/PR.
findings.impact: Full compromise of CI environment, potential secret exfiltration from GitHub Actions secrets.
findings.recommendation: Pin to SHA: uses: oven-sh/setup-bun@4bc47a7 (or current SHA from actions/runner-images). Run `gh api repos/oven-sh/setup-bun/commits/v2 | jq '.sha'` to get it.

findings.title: NEXT_PUBLIC_WEBHOOK_URL exposes Google Apps Script endpoint in JS bundle
findings.description: NEXT_PUBLIC_ prefix embeds the value in the client-side bundle. Anyone loading the page can find the Apps Script URL in page source. Currently broken (env var named WEBHOOK_URL, not NEXT_PUBLIC_WEBHOOK_URL) so not yet active.
findings.exploit_scenario: When the env var is correctly set for deployment, any user of the app can find the Apps Script URL and POST fake sent-email records to the Google Sheet, corrupting outreach tracking.
findings.impact: Google Sheet CRM data corrupted with fake sent-email records. Potential data exfiltration if the Apps Script has broader Sheets access.
findings.recommendation: Move webhook call to a server-side API route (POST /api/leads/{id}/send already exists — add the Sheet logging there). Use WEBHOOK_URL without NEXT_PUBLIC prefix so it stays server-side only.
